title: leontopodium seorakensis
intro: Leontopodium seorakensis, also known as Seorak Mountain Edelweiss, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the Korean Peninsula. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving wound healing, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In traditional Korean medicine, Leontopodium seorakensis is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
